description: Smart, funny love stories where pregnancy and growing up collide with family mess and sudden adulthood.
Brutal, landscape-heavy survival tales built from real events—where faith, brutality, and endurance grind together.
representative_movies: War for the Planet of the Apes
In the Heart of the Sea

description: Dating comedies where friends turn into competitors and attraction becomes a high-stakes prank.
Superhero action powered by secret identities, multiversal/legacy stakes, and the constant pressure of saving a city while your life fractures.
High-speed crime stories where car culture meets police pressure, revenge plots, and big, reckless set pieces.
Serious crime stories where violence, institutions, and personal trauma corrode everyone in the frame.
Supernatural terror that targets families—often through a child, a home, and a slow, escalating dread.
Speculative futures where robots and AI chase meaning—through loneliness, love, and the ethics of becoming human.
Movies where relationships—especially family—collide with talent, identity, and life choices, mixing comedy with bruised sincerity.
High-energy comedy-crime where mismatched cops (or cop-adjacent losers) stumble into investigations with punchlines and mayhem.
Psychological thrillers where a missing person or hidden double life detonates inside intimate relationships.
Ensemble capers where a mastermind crew outsmarts rivals, regulators, and each other using misdirection and investigation.
representative_movies: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows

description: Animated or live-action family fantasies that turn classic fairy tales into big, singable kingdoms of magic and romance.
Creature-feature terror set in claustrophobic ocean environments, where survival depends on grit, luck, and a shrinking air supply.
Horror films where teenagers or groups run the gauntlet of a killer, often spiked with revenge, dark humor, and rule-breaking supernatural menace.
Whimsical family comedies where a single parent’s life gets hijacked—usually by a nanny or stand-in caregiver—and everything spirals into heartfelt chaos.
High-intensity war films built on real missions—snipers, raids, and special-ops operations—driven by suspense and moral pressure.
representative_movies: 13 Hours: The Secret Soldiers of Benghazi

description: Swashbuckling globe-trotting adventures where archaeologists and treasure hunters chase riddles, relics, and curses across exotic ruins and seas.
representative_movies: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider - The Cradle of Life
Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
The Mummy: Tomb of the Dragon Emperor
Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales

description: Sharp, music-friendly high-school comedies where social status games and teenage crushes collide with coming-of-age awkwardness.
Future-shock action thrillers where artificial intelligence, cyborg bodies, and brutal systems turn survival into a philosophy fight.
recommended_label: Cyber Dystopia Action (AI, Cyborgs, Futures Gone Wrong)

description: High-speed policing with mismatched chemistry, where the job gets done and the punches come with punchlines.
Bright, fast-moving animated worlds where a charming misfit (often a villain) turns into an unlikely hero for the whole family.
Sweat-soaked fight dramas where mentorship, discipline, and heartbreak build toward the next bell.
Fairy-tale worlds—sometimes familiar, sometimes flipped—where magic, queens, hunts, and wonder collide in grand adventure.
representative_movies: The Chronicles of Narnia: Prince Caspian
The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader

description: Teen and young-adult romance stories built for emotional impact—secrets, fate, and heartbreak delivered with big, heartfelt sincerity.
High-stakes voyages where astronauts (and scientists) collide with time-bending mysteries, alien signals, and the physics of survival.
Tense cat-and-mouse crime stories where a killer’s plan unspools across transit hubs and crowded cities.
Unsettling horror where relationships fray into paranoia, and fear spreads like a contagion through households and trust.
Bright, family-friendly animated follow-ups packed with new villains, mid-film momentum, and comedy-forward adventure set pieces.
representative_movies: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs 2

description: Post-apocalyptic horror where outbreaks, conspiracies, and mutated threats force characters into desperate survival missions.
representative_movies: Resident Evil: Welcome to Raccoon City

description: Young heroes step into magic-soaked worlds where destiny, quests, and old-school wonder collide.
representative_movies: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
Miss Peregrine's Home for Peculiar Children

description: Big divine premises meet fast jokes—faith, ego, and love get scrambled in a comedic afterlife of consequences.
High-energy expeditions where paleontology, DNA tinkering, and island thrills turn into full-on dinosaur mayhem.
Tense space-bound hunts where deadly extraterrestrial creatures turn every firefight into a survival horror sprint.
Big ensemble superhero adventures where stakes balloon fast and set-piece action is built for maximum crowd payoff.
Bright, mid-30s romantic chaos where love, identity, and family plans collide—often with an unexpected pregnancy or parenthood question.
representative_movies: Bridget Jones: The Edge of Reason
To All the Boys I've Loved Before
Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again
To All the Boys: P.S. I Still Love You

description: Thoughtful, stylish romance stories where people fall into—then analyze—their feelings while cities and time do the emotional choreography.
representative_movies: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

description: Razor-paced action spy yarns where the central trick is infiltration—disguises, false identities, and secret agencies turning every plan into a new problem.
representative_movies: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One

description: Intense character studies of ambition and self-destruction, where music culture and addiction turn everyday life into a high-volume tragedy.
Fast, funny romance stories where chance encounters in big-city life spiral into feelings—often with weddings, friends, and public embarrassment as the accelerant.
representative_movies: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days

description: Tense, immersive World War II stories that track soldiers through fear, chaos, and moral pressure in real-time battle conditions.
representative_movies: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas

description: Supernatural horror built around haunting entities—often through dolls, spirits, and escalation from omen to possession.
Fast-talking, action-forward spy stories where the mission is mostly a setup for absurdity, pratfalls, and escalating criminal chaos.
representative_movies: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!

description: Dark crime thrillers that obsess over investigators and predators, where identity fractures under the weight of serial murder.
Big-screen mutant superhero adventures where powers, identity, and team politics collide in high-stakes action set pieces.
When the skyline becomes a boxing ring, giant creatures duke it out in loud, high-stakes spectacle.
representative_movies: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire

description: Tight, suspenseful stories where time travel and future technology turn every decision into a trap.
High-velocity crime action where revenge and assassin professionalism power the set pieces—often amplified in sequels.
A modern Western mood where cartels, borders, and brutality squeeze characters into irreversible choices.
Christmas chaos where family trouble and home/holiday trespass turn into slapstick action set pieces.
representative_movies: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York

description: Animated journeys where friendship and found-family power a bright, emotionally safe adventure.
representative_movies: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World

description: Biographical, based-on-real-events dramas where institutions, law, and power collide under pressure.
Big-screen sci-fi action with aliens, robots, and franchise-scale set pieces—built for momentum over subtlety.
Crime stories with bite—often revenge-driven—where menace and dark humor share the same spotlight.
A YA fantasy school-and-stakes run where growing up at a magical academy turns into a fight for survival.
representative_movies: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1
Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire

description: Vampire wars and slayer myth meets hard-edged action in rain-soaked, gothic cityscapes.
A serial-killer’s ruleset turns captivity into escalating puzzles where survival is the only answer.
Leashed by politics and deadlines, a world-traveling agent turns espionage into nonstop pursuit and retaliation.
When the universe shows up, the only plan is to survive the creature and the unknown rules it brings.
A fractured identity sparks a chase through assassins, agencies, and conspiracies where every lead comes with a flashback cost.
representative_movies: The Man in the Iron Mask

description: Big-planet mythmaking where Jedi ideals collide with empires, prophecy, and high-stakes space battles.
representative_movies: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith
Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones

description: Planet-scale calamity—natural or extraterrestrial—where survival becomes a countdown and big heroes try to out-run the inevitable.
representative_movies: The Day the Earth Stood Still

description: Horror built on foreknowledge—characters glimpse their deaths and race (usually too late) to dodge a supernatural timetable.
Rowdy, risk-light comedy sequels where friend groups spiral through weddings, road trips, and workplace mayhem for maximum laughs.
Young protagonists navigate brutal futures where survival is gamified—then pivot into revolution.
Big-ego friendships and bad-timing dates collide with office life, turning awkward romance into high-volume punchlines.
Heroic warriors, mythic creatures, and biblical/ancient legends fuel thunderous battles and stylized mythmaking.
Horror that knows the rules—then weaponizes them—using meta jokes, serial-killer setups, and franchise-baiting sequels.
Stylish, tense crime stories—gangsters, detectives, and hustlers—built from momentum, moral rot, and razor-edged dialogue.
representative_movies: Once Upon a Time in America

description: Superhero myths staged as big, conflicted battles—where identities, powers, and alliances fracture under moral intensity.
representative_movies: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice

description: Supernatural horror where the threat feels immediate—ghosts, demons, and possessions creeping in through ordinary spaces and escalating set pieces.
Teen romance driven by supernatural creatures—vampires, werewolves, and fate—where the emotional stakes are as sharp as the supernatural ones.
representative_movies: The Twilight Saga: Breaking Dawn - Part 1
The Twilight Saga: Breaking Dawn - Part 2

description: High-velocity spy and hitman thrillers where intelligence agencies, revenge missions, and shootout set pieces collide.
Romantic stories for younger audiences that borrow fairytale or gothic mood—often with real-world awkwardness, scandal, or a faintly unsettling edge.
recommended_label: Teen romance in gothic-ish modern fairytales

description: Emotional coming-of-age stories where friendship, school life, family ties, and identity shifts carry the weight.
representative_movies: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
Me and Earl and the Dying Girl
